metadata.purpose: Guide Claude Code through repository cleanup and the next experimentally reusable retrieval sprint.
metadata.northStar: Identify datasets that are experimentally reusable for a target scientific analysis, not merely topically similar.

context.summary: The repository already contains ontology matching, hybrid search, graph infrastructure, field embeddings, analysis affordances, source quality scoring, benchmark runners, hard-negative queries, dataset cards, and whitepaper artifacts. The next phase should reduce sprawl, strengthen provenance-backed claims, build executable affordance validation, and create a harder reusability benchmark.
context.keyProblem: Current benchmarks are useful but not hard enough to prove that embeddings, graph context, affordances, and hard-negative filtering improve scientific retrieval. Ablations show some components have marginal or inconsistent impact. Build evaluation that tests experimental reusability, not only label overlap.
context.anchorExample: The EBRAINS GUI search for "delay discounting" returns lexical matches involving delayed reach-to-grasp, reward/motivation mentions, and signal propagation delay. Neural Search should distinguish true delay discounting/intertemporal choice from motor delay, working-memory delay, and signal propagation delay.

workflowRules.rule: Use small commits with descriptive messages.
Do not add broad feature sprawl unless it directly supports experimental reusability ranking or evidence-backed explanation.
Do not prioritize frontend polish unless required to expose evidence fields.
Do not chase unrelated snapshot failures unless they block the deliverables.
Document starting test and lint status before changing architecture.
Keep claims honest. Mark speculative ideas as proposed or future work.

phase.name: Repository cleanup and canonical map
phase.goal: Make the repository clean, navigable, and safe to hand off.

tasks.task: Run git status, pytest, and ruff to capture starting state.
Remove __pycache__, *.pyc, .pytest_cache, .ruff_cache, .mypy_cache, node_modules, frontend dist, Python dist/build artifacts, root-level zip packages, and other generated clutter.
Consolidate docs: keep canonical docs in docs/ and move clearly superseded historical task prompts or handoff docs into archive_docs/ with an index.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Clean export contains no .git, node_modules, caches, pyc files, build artifacts, or root-level zip packages.
docs/CLEANUP_LOG.md records what changed and why.
Canonical documentation is easy to find.

phase.name: Reusability claim schema
phase.goal: Make provenance-backed claims the atomic unit of search, graph edges, and explanations.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Existing extracted labels can be represented as claims.
Claims serialize to JSONL.
Graph edges and result explanations can reference claim IDs.
Tests cover validation and serialization.

phase.name: Executable affordance validators
phase.goal: Convert analysis affordances from loose tags into validators with requirements, blockers, confidence, and evidence.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Validators run over normalized records and claims.
Missing requirements are explicit.
Hard blockers prevent high-confidence matches.
Tests include true positives, partial support, unsupported records, and false-positive decoys.

phase.name: Query sense disambiguation
phase.goal: Avoid lexical false positives by identifying scientific senses of overloaded terms.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: delay discounting resolves to intertemporal choice / delayed reward valuation.
signal propagation delay, motor delay, and working-memory delay are recognized as negative senses for delay discounting queries.
Sense penalties or flags are integrated into retrieval/reranking traces.

phase.name: Reusability Gold v1 benchmark
phase.goal: Create a benchmark that evaluates experimental reusability, not only label overlap.

requirements.requirement: Include ambiguity, analysis affordance, cross-modal/cross-species, natural-language, and exact lookup categories.
Include must-have variables, should-have variables, and hard-negative senses.
Report hard-negative violation rate, claim support rate, missing requirement penalties, and graded NDCG.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Benchmark loads and validates.
At least 10 hard-negative traps exist.
At least 6 affordance-driven queries exist.

phase.name: Candidate generation and scientific reranking
phase.goal: Make retrieval architecture more interpretable by separating candidate generation from scientific reranking.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Candidate-generation traces are available.
Reranker feature contributions are visible.
Ablations can test candidate generators and reranker features separately.

phase.name: Evidence-backed result cards
phase.goal: Expose matched constructs, matched affordances, matched requirements, missing requirements, hard-negative flags, and claim-backed evidence in search results.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: API or CLI search results include evidence fields.
Top results cite supporting claim IDs.
Benchmark reports surface missing requirements and hard-negative flags.

phase.name: Whitepaper and claim ledger sync
phase.goal: Make all external claims accurate, reproducible, and caveated.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: No metric in the whitepaper lacks a source artifact and replication command.
Corpus size is consistent across README, paper, and reports.
Recall@10 is distinguished from Label Recall@10.
Embedding and graph claims are caveated if current ablations do not yet prove benefit.

suggestedCommits.commit: chore: clean generated artifacts and add export script
docs: consolidate project map and cleanup log
test: add reusability gold v1 benchmark fixtures
feat: add evidence-backed reranking result explanations
docs: sync whitepaper claims and claim ledger

doNotPrioritize.item: Large whitepaper claims before benchmark improvements
More generic embedding providers without per-field ablations
Blind corpus expansion without reusability labels
A full learned reranker before interpretable features and labels are trustworthy
